title: Remove pollen pack purchase bonuses
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
Removes the bonus that pack purchases granted on top of the paid amount. You now get exactly what you buy: $5 → 5 Pollen, $100 → 100 Pollen.

- Drop `bonusPollen`/`pollenGrant` from the pack catalog; `amountUsd` is the single source of truth
- Checkout metadata slimmed to `packKey` + `packAmountUsd`; the purchase webhook and auto-top-up now credit `amountUsd`
- Remove the `+X% bonus` badge, "FREE" labels, and beta-bonus copy from the dashboard slider and Stripe checkout
- Strip bonus columns (Pack Mix, Blended Burn) from the Pollen Flow dashboard and fall back to the base pack amount; historical `packPollenGrant` events stay accurate
- Update tests

Follow-up: the now-unused `pollen_grant` column is dropped in a separate PR after this deploys — prod runs migrations before deploying the worker, so dropping a NOT NULL column the old worker still uses would break auto-top-up.

gist.headline: Straightforward Pollen Packs
gist.blurb: We've simplified our Pollen packs to a straightforward 1:1 ratio. What you see is exactly what you get—no complicated math required!
gist.summary: Pollen pack bonuses have been removed, establishing a direct 1:1 ratio where $10 credits exactly 10 Pollen instead of the previous bonus amounts.
gist.impact: Users purchasing Pollen packs will now receive an exact 1:1 USD-to-Pollen conversion, and the checkout UI has been streamlined to remove bonus badges and beta labels.
gist.visual_concept: The bee mascot organizing or counting pollen coins/credits on a simple scale or abacus, representing straightforward 1:1 math. A clean, simple transaction.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny accountant visor, happily organizing glowing yellow pollen coins on a simple wooden scale. A chunky retro cash register sits nearby with a lime green (#ecf874) screen showing '1:1'. Soft pastel gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley, emotionally warm.
